item.title: Re: Photos
item.content:encoded:
Hi Voct! Here are my image disclosures:

  • The (new) mosquito image is CC: https://ccsearch.creativecommons.org/photos/2d457c75-a5d3-4dc0-9da1-bd52b535ca9c
  • The "Oscar" image is a modified image from a Public Domain pack of pre-1900s mugshots, and is considered public domain.

item.title: Re: Photos
item.content:encoded: Suggested CC replacement for the mosquito pic (with the bug on the left cropped out).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like the last interview log because of its general unimportance in the overall tone of the article. I don't know, it feels kind of tacked on. No vote from me.

item.title: Photos
item.content:encoded:
The mosquito pic seems to trace back to Getty Images and, my guess, will have to be replaced.
https://www.gettyimages.ca/detail/photo/female-mosquito-biting-high-res-stock-photography/129837019

The mugshot, I can't find yet; it's labeled as from "Franklin County", but twenty-five states have a county named that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's an interesting idea, but I feel like the charm of this one is that anyone bitten ends up an exact duplicate of Mr. Peleschak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I read 'Known Individuals affected', I kinda hoped it would read Oscar Peleschak.
But then again, that would be too joke-y

Also, nice skip, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't help but wonder what happens if you get a blood transfusion from Oscar Prime

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My God I hate bugs! I hate what these ones do even more. Take my +1 for your research toward neutralizing these things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only other one (that comes to mind) that literally turns you into the person is SCP-2009.

But there's also SCP-2310 and SCP-2150 which create mental copies of people. There might be some in Series I and II, but at the time I cannot recall them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it just me, or do we have several things what make clones of someone already? None of their numbers come to mind, but nothing here really felt fresh to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Soooo… was the interview meant to be deleted when the image processing team came through?

Edit: Note to self, go straight to 17 next time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted not just for the concept but because this is not what I expected when I read the words "Oscar Bug" on the main list. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this kind of fucking stuff

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The context is that the researcher conducting the interview is also cleared to work on some other insect SCPs. That's it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Down vote because the cross links don't seem related.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually like the idea of a non-anomalous human who "lives forever" so-to-speak from something like this. Reminds me of the Bifurcating Man, although they have vastly different effects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Or smaller Peleschaks.

When I read the effects produced by the bite, I couldn't stop laughing. This is seriously amazing, I never expected that. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Delightful levels of WTF in this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I laughed so hard at this. have you considered writing a tale about it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the implication that Peleschak's hometown was at least partially aware of the wierdness, and was doing their best to just sort of ignore it.

sherriff: Jimmy Smith got Oscar-ed last night.
mayor: Dammit! That's four Oscars this month…and Jimmy was on my bowling team. What are we going to do now, Oscars can't pick up a 7-10 to save his life

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know what it is about this… But I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find this remarkably terrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Complete transformation into a human.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, wait, wait… So this thing could turn a cow in Oscar Peleschak? Would it be a Peleschak cow, or a complete transformation into a human?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"What's this?"

*sigh* "Another Peleschak foot. Chalk down a left, [REDACTED]."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this concept. But I think this needs a bit more narrative in order to be truly creepy.

Who is Oscar—why would he / anybody want clones of him? Or is this supposed to be natural? Guessing at that question doesn't creep me out.

Vote withheld, for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This IS the Foundation, we might have different Hazmat suits then normal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I live in the US. In the US., hazmat gear is rated A and B. please see http://www.sccfd.org/clothing_hazmat.html.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
2) If this thing affects mammals like cats and mice now, and it still only takes 24 hours, you should probably mention something about how a housecat gains 60 kg or so in 24 hours, while comatose, without violating the laws of thermodynamics.

To solve this, mention that critters without sufficient mass will become incomplete copies of Mr. Peleschak, maybe even mention that the critters don't survive the partial transformation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Retracting my downvote on the basis of it no longer only feeding on humans. Insects that only target humans are one of my major pet peeves, since I can't imagine any insect evolving that way.

Two new nitpicks, though:

1) There's no such thing as a "Level A hazardous materials suit". Biohazard safety levels, in order from no containment to "negative-pressurized rooms and full-body suits with external oxygen supply" containment, are Level 0, Level 2, Level 3, and Level 4. You probably meant Level 4.

2) If this thing affects mammals like cats and mice now, and it still only takes 24 hours, you should probably mention something about how a housecat gains 60 kg or so in 24 hours, while comatose, without violating the laws of thermodynamics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interview log is really predictable, it could be alot more cryptic. No vote for now, upvote if interview is improved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not really, but it seemed like something that would be included. Remember that Level 2's have a list of the SCP's they are allowed to interact with after them in their clearance level (i.e., level 2\xxx-yyy-zzz). However, I never see reference to level 2 foundation members, so I did what I thought would make sense for a file such as this.

I selected two articles that I thought a single researcher might work on. they are all insect-related.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Um. I may be missing something here, but do the crosslinks serve any purpose at all?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was expecting a 439 ripoff, but then I read further. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God damn it now theres a mosqito in my room and its making me paranoid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're welcome, Mr. Peleschak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted because 1.) this is really good and 2.) I just killed a mosquito on my computer screen while I was reading this and I'm not sure if it bit me. Also, I live in Massachusetts.

Thanks for the serious paranoia fuel!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would be really good if you had hints of what happens when somtehing smaller than a human is 'infected', like a rabbit becoming a head or something of that nature (can you imagine walking into a forest and seeing a bunch of half-formed people screaming as they wiggle on the ground?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know what? I don't see why it wouldn't work on animals. Maybe limit it to mammals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this but it would be better if it also changed animals and stuff into the same guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Bugs! EWWWWWWWWWWWW!"

Seriously, though - this is better than the standard insect-body-horror SCP, in that it has an original effect rather than being a 439 ripoff. Still, it's just another "bugs that only feed on humans and transform them into something weird" article of the type we've had like a dozen of, so I'm downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is after a self-delete of old article, with a full rewrite and a better hook. Thanks to that_tall_fellow for help with the idea.

2020 image disclosure edit

  • The (new) mosquito image is CC: https://ccsearch.creativecommons.org/photos/17aaa74d-7287-4235-b3c9-e406425502c7
  • The "Oscar" image is a modified image from a Public Domain pack of pre-1900s mugshots courtesy of Ampersand Galleries, and is considered public domain (a) under the pre-1924 rule and (b) as released into the Public Domain by Oregon State Police's public records guidelines
